---
title: "Analysis2219_21_36"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-25"
---

```{R}
library(arrow)

library(tidyverse)

library(gtools)

library(ggplot2)

library(ggrepel)

library(patchwork)

library(limma)

library(msqrob2)

library(QFeatures)

library(pheatmap)

library(viridis)

library(readxl)

library(variancePartition)

library(Rtsne)

library(tibble)
 
```

```{r}
# Variables
CMBnr <- "CMB-2216"
cells <- "NCI-H1650"
output_folder <- paste0("results/",CMBnr)
if (!dir.exists(output_folder)) {
  dir.create(output_folder)
}
passage_baseline  <- "P0"   # Reference passage
passage_of_interest <- "P12"  # Passage to compare against baseline
tag <- paste0(passage_of_interest, "_vs_", passage_baseline, "_", CMBnr)
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================

# 1. LOAD EXPERIMENTAL DESIGN

# =============================================================================

exp_design <- read.csv(paste0("Data/",CMBnr,"/Experimental_design_", CMBnr,".csv"), sep = ";") %>%
  dplyr::select(Sample, Group, Replicate, Run) %>%

  mutate(

    passage_nr = as.integer(str_extract(Group, "[0-9]+$"))-1, #-1 as passage mistakenly starts at 1 in experimental design file

    passage    = paste0("P", passage_nr)

  )
 
cat("Experimental Design (lookup-tabel):\n")

print(exp_design, n = Inf)

```

```{r}
# =============================================================================

# 2. READ PARQUET AND FILTER

# =============================================================================
 
# READ PARQUET

raw <- read_parquet(paste0("Data/",CMBnr,"/report.parquet"))
 
raw_filtered <- raw %>%

  dplyr::filter(Q.Value    <= 0.01,

                PG.Q.Value <= 0.01,
                
                !grepl("cRAP", Protein.Group),
                
                !grepl("cRAP", Protein.Ids)
                )%>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Keep only the lead ID
    Protein.Group = sub(";.*", "", Protein.Group),
    
    Protein.Ids   = sub(";.*", "", Protein.Ids),
  )
 
cat(sprintf("\nRows after FDR- en cRAP-filter: %d (van %d)\n", nrow(raw_filtered), nrow(raw)))

```

```{r}
# =============================================================================

# 3. Preprocessing & Protein Aggregation

# =============================================================================
# join the data
raw_filtered_annotated <- raw_filtered %>%
  inner_join(exp_design, by = "Run")

# define factor levels
sample_levels <- mixedsort(unique(raw_filtered_annotated$Sample))

# apply the factor
raw_filtered_annotated$Sample <- factor(raw_filtered_annotated$Sample, 
                                        levels = sample_levels)

# reshape the data
raw_wide <- raw_filtered_annotated %>%
  select(Precursor.Id, Protein.Ids, Sample, Genes, Precursor.Quantity) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Sample, 
    values_from = Precursor.Quantity
  ) %>%
  select(Precursor.Id, Protein.Ids, Genes, all_of(as.character(sample_levels)))

# identify which columns in diann_wide are the sample intensities
intensity_cols <- exp_design$Sample

# create the object
pe <- readQFeatures(
  raw_wide, 
  ecol = intensity_cols, 
  name = "precursor_raw", 
  fnames = "Precursor.Id"
  
)

```

```{r}
# filtering NA treshhold 70%
rowData(pe[["precursor_raw"]])$nValid <- rowSums(!is.na(assay(pe[["precursor_raw"]])))
threshold <- ncol(assay(pe[["precursor_raw"]])) * 0.7

# calculate number of NAs per row
n_na <- ncol(pe[["precursor_raw"]]) - rowData(pe[["precursor_raw"]])$nValid

# plot the histogram
hist(n_na, 
     breaks = ncol(pe[["precursor_raw"]]), 
     col = "steelblue", 
     main = "Distribution of Missing Values",
     xlab = "Number of Samples Missing Precursor", 
     ylab = "Frequency (Number of Precursors)",
     xlim = c(0, 50)
       )

# 70% threshold line
abline(v = ncol(pe[["precursor_raw"]]) * 0.3, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
```

```{r warning=FALSE}
# apply filter
pe <- filterFeatures(pe, ~ nValid >= threshold)

# log transform (base 2)
pe <- logTransform(pe, i = "precursor_raw", name = "precursor_log")

# normalize (median centering)
pe <- normalize(pe, i = "precursor_log", name = "precursor_norm", method = "center.median")

# aggregate precursors to protein level
pe <- aggregateFeatures(
  pe, 
  i = "precursor_norm", 
  fcol = "Protein.Ids", 
  name = "protein",
  fun = MsCoreUtils::robustSummary
)

# Identify the number of proteins in the aggregated assay
num_proteins <- nrow(pe[["protein"]])

cat("Number of unique proteins after aggregation:", num_proteins, "\n")
```

```{r}
# reorder the design
correct_order <- match(colnames(pe[["precursor_raw"]]), exp_design$Sample)
ordered_design <- exp_design[correct_order, ]

# Set the row names of the metadata to match the sample names
rownames(ordered_design) <- ordered_design$Sample

# Assign to colData
colData(pe) <- DataFrame(ordered_design)

# Verify
head(colData(pe))
```

```{r}
# Passage as factor
pe$passage <- factor(pe$passage, levels = mixedsort(unique(pe$passage)))

head(colData(pe))
```
```{r}
# Compare distributions of log-transformed vs. normalized data
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Before: Log-transformed only
limma::plotDensities(assay(pe[["precursor_log"]]), 
                     main = "Before Normalisation", legend = FALSE)

# After: Median Centered
limma::plotDensities(assay(pe[["precursor_norm"]]), 
                     main = "After Normalisation", legend = FALSE)
```


```{r}
# Boxplots for all 39 samples
boxplot(assay(pe[["precursor_norm"]]), 
        main = "Normalized Intensities", 
        las = 2, col = "skyblue", cex.axis = 0.7)
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lwd = 2) # The median should sit on 0
```

```{R}
# =============================================================================

# 4. PCA PLOTS

# =============================================================================
# Create matrix for PCAs

mat_pca   <- assay(pe, "protein")
 
# Impute NA's

mat_pca_imp <- apply(mat_pca, 2, function(x) {

  x[is.na(x)] <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE); x

})
 
# PCA

pca     <- prcomp(t(mat_pca_imp), scale. = TRUE, center = TRUE)

pct_var <- round(summary(pca)$importance[2, ] * 100, 1)
 
pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca$x) %>%

  rownames_to_column("Sample") %>%

  left_join(dplyr::select(exp_design, Sample, passage, Replicate),

            by = "Sample")
 
# color palette

nrofpassages <- max(exp_design$passage_nr, na.rm = TRUE)
pal_passages <- setNames(scales::hue_pal()((nrofpassages+2)), paste0("P", 0:(nrofpassages+1)))

#labeling 

passage_levels <- paste0("P", 0:(nrofpassages+1))

pca_df$passage <- factor(pca_df$passage, levels = passage_levels)
```

```{r}
# PC1 vs PC2

p_pca12 <- ggplot(pca_df,

                  aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = passage, label = paste0(passage, ".", Replicate))) +

  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.9) +

  geom_text_repel(size = 2.8, max.overlaps = 30, show.legend = FALSE) +

  stat_ellipse(aes(group = passage), type = "norm",

               linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.35, show.legend = FALSE) +

  scale_color_manual(values = pal_passages, name = "Passage") +

  labs(

    title    = paste0("PCA – ", cells),

    subtitle = sprintf("%d proteins  |  %d samples",

                       nrow(mat_pca), ncol(mat_pca)),

    x = sprintf("PC1 (%.1f%%)", pct_var[1]),

    y = sprintf("PC2 (%.1f%%)", pct_var[2])

  ) +

  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +

  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "right")

p_pca12

```

```{r}
# PC1 vs PC3

p_pca13 <- ggplot(pca_df,

                  aes(x = PC1, y = PC3, color = passage, label = paste0(passage, ".", Replicate))) +

  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.9) +

  geom_text_repel(size = 2.8, max.overlaps = 30, show.legend = FALSE) +

  stat_ellipse(aes(group = passage), type = "norm",

               linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.35, show.legend = FALSE) +

  scale_color_manual(values = pal_passages, name = "Passage") +

  labs(

    title    = paste0("PCA – ", cells),

    subtitle = sprintf("%d proteins  |  %d samples",

                       nrow(mat_pca), ncol(mat_pca)),
    x = sprintf("PC1 (%.1f%%)", pct_var[1]),

    y = sprintf("PC3 (%.1f%%)", pct_var[3])) +

  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +

  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "right")

p_pca13

```

```{r}
# scree plot

scree_df <- data.frame(

  PC  = factor(paste0("PC", 1:10), levels = paste0("PC", 1:10)),

  Var = pct_var[1:10]

)

p_scree <- ggplot(scree_df, aes(x = PC, y = Var)) +

  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.8) +

  geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.8) +

  geom_point(color = "firebrick", size = 2) +

  labs(title = paste0("Scree plot ", cells), x = NULL, y = "Variance explained (%)") +

  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +

  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p_scree

```


```{r}
# get loadings of PCs 1, 2, and 3

pc1_vals <- pca$rotation[, "PC1"]

pc1_drivers_high <- head(sort(pc1_vals, decreasing = TRUE), 10)
pc1_drivers_low <- head(sort(pc1_vals, decreasing = FALSE), 10)

pc2_vals <- pca$rotation[, "PC2"]
pc2_drivers_high <- head(sort(pc2_vals, decreasing = TRUE), 10)
pc2_drivers_low <- head(sort(pc2_vals, decreasing = FALSE), 10)

pc3_vals <- pca$rotation[, "PC3"]
pc3_drivers_high <- head(sort(pc3_vals, decreasing = TRUE), 10)
pc3_drivers_low <- head(sort(pc3_vals, decreasing = FALSE), 10)

all_drivers_pc1 <- c(names(pc1_drivers_high), names(pc1_drivers_low))
all_drivers_pc2 <- c(names(pc2_drivers_high), names(pc2_drivers_low))
all_drivers_pc3 <- c(names(pc3_drivers_high), names(pc3_drivers_low))

# save pc drivers to txt
writeLines(all_drivers_pc1, con = paste0(output_folder, "/driverspc1_", CMBnr, ".txt"))
writeLines(all_drivers_pc2, con = paste0(output_folder, "/driverspc2_", CMBnr, ".txt"))
writeLines(all_drivers_pc3, con = paste0(output_folder, "/driverspc3_", CMBnr, ".txt"))

```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
 
# 5. tSNE PLOT
 
# =============================================================================

n_samples      <- ncol(mat_pca_imp)
perplexity_val <- 10
 
set.seed(42)
tsne_result <- Rtsne(t(mat_pca_imp), 
                     perplexity = perplexity_val, 
                     verbose    = TRUE,
                     pca        = TRUE,   
                     check_duplicates = FALSE)
 
tsne_df <- data.frame(
  tSNE1 = tsne_result$Y[, 1],
  tSNE2 = tsne_result$Y[, 2]
) %>%
  mutate(Sample = colnames(mat_pca_imp)) %>%
  left_join(dplyr::select(exp_design, Sample, passage, Replicate), by = "Sample")
 
tsne_df$passage <- factor(tsne_df$passage, levels = passage_levels)
 
p_tsne <- ggplot(tsne_df, aes(x = tSNE1, y = tSNE2, 
                               color = passage, 
                               label = paste0(passage, ".", Replicate))) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 2.8, max.overlaps = 30, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_passages, name = "Passage") +
  labs(
    title    = paste0("tSNE – ", cells),
    subtitle = sprintf("%d proteins  |  %d samples",
                       nrow(mat_pca_imp), ncol(mat_pca_imp)),
    x = "tSNE 1",
    y = "tSNE 2"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "right")
 
p_tsne
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================

# 6. Variance Partition

# =============================================================================
# prepare data for variancePartition
metadata_xl <- read_excel(paste0("Data/",CMBnr,"/extra_metadata.xlsx"))

mat_vp <- mat_pca_imp
info_vp <- exp_design %>%
  left_join(metadata_xl, by = "passage_nr")

# set the row names using the 'sample' column
rownames(info_vp) <- info_vp$Sample

# remove the 'sample' column so only numeric data remains
info_vp$Sample <- NULL

info_vp <- info_vp %>%
  mutate(across(c(passage, operator, splitting_ratio), as.factor))

```


```{r}
# define formula for variancePartition
form <- ~ (1 | passage) + (1 | operator) + (1 | splitting_ratio)

```

```{r}
varPart <- fitExtractVarPartModel(mat_vp, form, info_vp)

# sort variables (i.e. columns) by median fraction of variance explained
vp <- sortCols(varPart)

# violin plot of contribution of each variable to total variance
vpplot <- plotVarPart(vp)

# add title
vpplot <- vpplot + 
  ggtitle(paste0("Variance Partition ", cells)) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 18, face = "bold"))

vpplot
```
```{r}
# Calculate the median percentage of variance explained for each variable
vp_summary <- colMeans(varPart) * 100
vp_medians <- apply(varPart, 2, median) * 100

# Combine into a clean table for your thesis
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(vp_summary),
  Median_Percent = round(vp_medians, 2)
) %>%
  arrange(desc(Median_Percent))

print(summary_table)
```

```{r warning=FALSE}
# =============================================================================

# 7. Differential Expression Analysis

# =============================================================================
# --- TREND ANALYSIS BRANCH ---
pe_trend <- pe
pe_trend <- msqrob(pe_trend, i = "protein", formula = ~passage_nr, overwrite = TRUE)
L_trend <- makeContrast("passage_nr = 0", 
                        parameterNames = colnames(model.matrix(~passage_nr, colData(pe_trend))))
pe_trend <- hypothesisTest(pe_trend, i = "protein", contrast = L_trend)

# --- PAIRWISE ANALYSIS BRANCH ---
pe_pair <- pe
pe_pair <- msqrob(pe_pair, i = "protein", formula = ~passage, overwrite = TRUE)

contrast_name <- paste0("passage", passage_of_interest)
contrast_str  <- paste0(contrast_name, " = 0")

L_pair <- makeContrast(contrast_str,
                       parameterNames = colnames(model.matrix(~passage, colData(pe_pair))))
pe_pair <- hypothesisTest(pe_pair, i = "protein", contrast = L_pair)

```


```{r}
# Extract Pairwise
gene_lookup <- as.data.frame(rowData(pe_pair[[1]])) %>% 
  dplyr::select(Protein.Ids, Genes) %>%
  distinct()

res_pair <- rowData(pe_pair[["protein"]])[[contrast_name]] %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Protein.Ids") %>%
  left_join(gene_lookup, by = "Protein.Ids") %>%
  mutate(Genes = ifelse(is.na(Genes) | Genes == "", Protein.Ids, Genes)) %>%
  mutate(Protein = Protein.Ids)

# --- VOLCANO PLOT ---
p_cutoff <- 0.05
fc_cutoff <- 1

plot_title <- paste("Volcano Plot:", cells, passage_of_interest, "vs", passage_baseline)

p_volcano <- ggplot(res_pair, aes(x = logFC, y = -log10(adjPval))) +
  geom_point(aes(color = (adjPval < p_cutoff & abs(logFC) > fc_cutoff)), alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey", "red")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-fc_cutoff, fc_cutoff), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(p_cutoff), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = res_pair %>% filter(adjPval < p_cutoff & abs(logFC) > fc_cutoff),
    aes(label = Genes),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = 12
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title = plot_title,
       x = "Log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(Adjusted P-value)",
       color = "Significant") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 15, face = "bold"))

print(p_volcano)
```

```{r}

# Extract Trend (Slope across all passages)
res_trend <- rowData(pe_trend[["protein"]])$passage_nr %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Protein.Ids") %>%
  left_join(gene_lookup, by = "Protein.Ids") %>%
  mutate(Genes = ifelse(is.na(Genes) | Genes == "", Protein.Ids, Genes))

# --- HEATMAP ---
top_trend_data <- res_trend %>%
  arrange(adjPval) %>%
  head(30)

top_trend_ids <- top_trend_data$Protein.Ids
top_gene_labels <- top_trend_data$Genes


heatmap_data   <- assay(pe_trend[["protein"]])[top_trend_ids, ]
colnames(heatmap_data) <- paste0("P", colData(pe_trend)$passage_nr,
                                  "_",
                                  sub(".*_", "", rownames(colData(pe_trend))))

heatmap_scaled <- t(scale(t(heatmap_data)))

heatmap_title <- paste(paste0("Top ", length(top_trend_ids) ," Proteins by Longitudinal Trend ", cells))

p_heatmap <- pheatmap(heatmap_scaled,
              cluster_cols = FALSE,
              show_colnames = TRUE,
              show_rownames = TRUE,
              labels_row = top_gene_labels,
              color = viridis(100),
              main = heatmap_title,
              border_color = NA,
              fontsize_row = 8)

```


```{r}
# --- Define Basic Significant Sets with Direction ---

# --- DIRECTIONAL PROTEIN SETS ---
trend_up   <- res_trend %>% filter(adjPval < 0.05, logFC > 0) %>% pull(Protein.Ids)
trend_down <- res_trend %>% filter(adjPval < 0.05, logFC < 0) %>% pull(Protein.Ids)
pair_up    <- res_pair  %>% filter(adjPval < 0.05, logFC >  fc_cutoff) %>% pull(Protein)
pair_down  <- res_pair  %>% filter(adjPval < 0.05, logFC < -fc_cutoff) %>% pull(Protein)

both_up        <- intersect(trend_up,   pair_up)
both_down      <- intersect(trend_down, pair_down)
all_sig <- unique(c(trend_up, trend_down, pair_up, pair_down))
  

background_list <- rownames(pe_pair[["protein"]])

print(paste0("# Both up: ", length(both_up)))
print(paste0("# Both down: ", length(both_down)))
print(paste0("# Pair up: ", length(pair_up)))
print(paste0("# Pair down: ", length(pair_down)))
print(paste0("# Trend up: ", length(trend_up)))
print(paste0("# Trend down: ", length(trend_down)))
print(paste0("# Total: ", length(all_sig)))
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================

# 8. Protein Expression Profile Plots

# =============================================================================

#--- Line Plot trend---

# Slice the top 10 rows (Unchanged)
top_10_data <- res_trend %>%
  arrange(adjPval) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  mutate(Genes = make.unique(Genes))

top_10_ids <- top_10_data$Protein.Ids
top_intensities <- assay(pe, "protein")[top_10_ids, ]

# Build the data frame
plot_data <- as.data.frame(top_intensities) %>%
  mutate(Protein.Ids = rownames(.)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Protein.Ids, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Intensity") %>%
  left_join(as.data.frame(colData(pe)), by = "Sample") %>%
  left_join(dplyr::select(top_10_data, Protein.Ids, Genes), by = "Protein.Ids")


unique_passages <- unique(plot_data$passage)
correct_order <- unique_passages[order(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9]", "", unique_passages)))]

plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(passage = factor(passage, levels = correct_order)) %>%
  group_by(Protein.Ids) %>%
  mutate(Z_Intensity = (Intensity - mean(Intensity, na.rm=TRUE)) / sd(Intensity, na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Generate the profile plot
p_unified <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = passage, y = Z_Intensity, 
                                   color = Genes, group = Protein.Ids)) + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = paste0("Protein Expression Profile of Top 10 Trend Proteins ", cells),
       subtitle = "Data Z-score scaled to show relative changes",
       x = "Passage",
       y = "Relative Expression",
       color = "Protein") + 
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

p_unified
```

```{r}
# --- Line Plot PC drivers---
driver_ids <- names(pc1_drivers_high)

driver_mapping <- data.frame(Protein.Ids = driver_ids) %>%
  left_join(gene_lookup, by = "Protein.Ids") %>%
  mutate(Genes = ifelse(is.na(Genes) | Genes == "", Protein.Ids, Genes)) %>%
  mutate(Genes = make.unique(Genes))

driver_intensities <- assay(pe, "protein")[driver_ids, ]

plot_data_drivers <- as.data.frame(driver_intensities) %>%
  mutate(Protein.Ids = rownames(.)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Protein.Ids, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Intensity") %>%
  left_join(as.data.frame(colData(pe)), by = "Sample") %>%
  left_join(driver_mapping, by = "Protein.Ids")

unique_passages_dr <- unique(plot_data_drivers$passage)
correct_order_dr <- unique_passages_dr[order(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9]", "", unique_passages_dr)))]

plot_data_drivers <- plot_data_drivers %>%
  mutate(passage = factor(passage, levels = correct_order_dr)) %>%
  group_by(Protein.Ids) %>%
  mutate(Z_Intensity = (Intensity - mean(Intensity, na.rm=TRUE)) / sd(Intensity, na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Generate the driver line plot
p_line_high <- ggplot(plot_data_drivers, aes(x = passage, y = Z_Intensity, 
                                             color = Genes, group = Protein.Ids)) + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = paste0("Protein Expression Profile of 10 Most Negative PC1 Drivers ", cells),
       subtitle = "Data Z-score scaled to show relative changes",
       x = "Passage",
       y = "Relative Expression",
       color = "Protein") + 
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

p_line_high
```

```{r}
# --- Line Plot PC drivers---
driver_ids <- names(pc1_drivers_low)

driver_mapping <- data.frame(Protein.Ids = driver_ids) %>%
  left_join(gene_lookup, by = "Protein.Ids") %>%
  mutate(Genes = ifelse(is.na(Genes) | Genes == "", Protein.Ids, Genes)) %>%
  mutate(Genes = make.unique(Genes))

driver_intensities <- assay(pe, "protein")[driver_ids, ]

plot_data_drivers <- as.data.frame(driver_intensities) %>%
  mutate(Protein.Ids = rownames(.)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Protein.Ids, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Intensity") %>%
  left_join(as.data.frame(colData(pe)), by = "Sample") %>%
  left_join(driver_mapping, by = "Protein.Ids")

unique_passages_dr <- unique(plot_data_drivers$passage)
correct_order_dr <- unique_passages_dr[order(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9]", "", unique_passages_dr)))]

plot_data_drivers <- plot_data_drivers %>%
  mutate(passage = factor(passage, levels = correct_order_dr)) %>%
  group_by(Protein.Ids) %>%
  mutate(Z_Intensity = (Intensity - mean(Intensity, na.rm=TRUE)) / sd(Intensity, na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Generate the driver line plot
p_line_low <- ggplot(plot_data_drivers, aes(x = passage, y = Z_Intensity, 
                                             color = Genes, group = Protein.Ids)) + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Paired") + 
  labs(title = paste0("Protein Expression Profile of 10 Most Negative PC1 Drivers ", cells),
       subtitle = "Data Z-score scaled to show relative changes",
       x = "Passage",
       y = "Relative Expression",
       color = "Protein") + 
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

p_line_low
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================

# 9. Exporting

# =============================================================================

# --- EXPORT TAG---
tag <- paste0(passage_of_interest, "_vs_", passage_baseline)

# --- DEFINE OUTPUT PATHS ---
base_dir  <- paste0("results/", CMBnr, "/", tag)
plot_dir  <- paste0(base_dir, "/plots")
dea_dir   <- paste0(base_dir, "/dea_lists")

# --- CREATE FOLDERS IF THEY DON'T EXIST ---
dir.create(plot_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(dea_dir,  recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# --- SAVING PLOTS ---
png(paste0(plot_dir, "/Volcano_", tag, ".png"),
    width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
print(p_volcano)
dev.off()

png(paste0(plot_dir, "/Heatmap_Trend_", CMBnr, ".png"),
    width = 12, height = 14, units = "in", res = 300)
print(p_heatmap)
dev.off()

ggsave(file.path(output_folder, paste0("pca12_", CMBnr, ".png")), p_pca12, width = 8, height = 7)
ggsave(file.path(output_folder, paste0("pca13_", CMBnr, ".png")), p_pca13, width = 8, height = 7)
ggsave(file.path(output_folder, paste0("scree_", CMBnr, ".png")), p_scree, width = 10, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(output_folder, paste0("vpplot_", CMBnr, ".png")), vpplot, width = 8, height = 7)
ggsave(file.path(output_folder, paste0("tsne_", CMBnr, ".png")), p_tsne, width = 8, height = 7)
ggsave(file.path(output_folder, paste0("top10protevo_", CMBnr, ".png")), p_unified, width = 10, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(output_folder, paste0("pchighevo_", CMBnr, ".png")), p_line_high, width = 10, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(output_folder, paste0("pclowevo_", CMBnr, ".png")), p_line_low, width = 10, height = 5)

cat(paste0("Plots saved as png\n"), create.dir = TRUE)

# --- SAVE DEA LISTS ---
writeLines(both_up,         paste0(dea_dir, "/Consistent_UP_",      tag, ".txt"))
writeLines(both_down,       paste0(dea_dir, "/Consistent_Down_",    tag, ".txt"))
writeLines(trend_up,   paste0(dea_dir, "/Trend_UP_",      tag, ".txt"))
writeLines(trend_down, paste0(dea_dir, "/Trend_DOWN_",    tag, ".txt"))
writeLines(pair_up,    paste0(dea_dir, "/Pair_UP_",       tag, ".txt"))
writeLines(pair_down,  paste0(dea_dir, "/Pair_DOWN_",     tag, ".txt"))
writeLines(all_sig,  paste0(dea_dir, "/All_Sig_",     tag, ".txt"))
writeLines(background_list, paste0(dea_dir, "/Study_Background_",   tag, ".txt"))

# --- SAVE PROTEIN INTENSITIES ---
# Extract the intensity matrix
export_table <- assay(pe, "protein")

# Save to CSV
write.csv(export_table, paste0("results/", CMBnr,"/Protein_Intensities_Wide.csv"), row.names = TRUE)

```
